summary: Set 7 Session 2: switched every state-reading site from file-presence branching to reading session-state.json's canonical `status`. Added read_status() (Python) / readStatus() (TS) with shared canonical-loader + lazy-synth fallback. The fallback uses _backfill_payload / backfillPayload inference so a legacy folder that slipped through Set 7 Session 1's backfill is classified by current file presence (change-log -> complete; activity-log -> in-progress; neither -> not-started) instead of being regressed to not-started. Tolerant alias canonicalization at the read boundary ("completed" -> "complete", "done" -> "complete") preserves the spec's "backfill leaves drifted files untouched" rule while preventing reader regressions on sets 005/006. The reconciler stranded-sweep, close-out gate idempotency check, and current_lifecycle_state were intentionally NOT collapsed: each operates on the events ledger (the authoritative source per existing codebase convention), and a coarse read_status pre-filter would either duplicate the events check or mask drift those readers exist to surface; documented inline. Cross-provider verification VERIFIED on round 3 after addressing two rounds of findings (1: read_status validation hole + missing tests; 2: lazy-synth misclassified legacy folders).

reason.specifics: Session 2 landed VERIFIED after three verifier rounds; same orchestrator has full context on the read_status helper, the lazy-synth-via-backfill-inference fallback, and the deliberate non-collapses (reconciler / gate idempotency / current_lifecycle_state) needed for Session 3's bootstrap-and-docs work.
